reason: Sense and Sensibility – love across class divides in Regency England
Romeo and Juliet – timeless tale of forbidden love and tragic longing
Jane Eyre – gothic romance of social barriers and inner yearning
Anna Karenina – epic passion clashes with 19th-century Russian society
Age of Innocence – aristocratic love tested by rigid social mores
Gone with the Wind – sweeping Civil War romance defying convention
Emma – spirited matchmaker challenges social expectations with charm
Persuasion – love lost and earned amidst rigid class structures
Legends of the Fall – forbidden loves tested by war and family duty
Pride & Prejudice – witty clash of social standing and mutual respect
